# KFC Card

## Summary
The `Kfc` card is a custom card definition that provides a representation of a KFC (Kentucky Fried Chicken) restaurant location. It can be used within a consuming card or field to display information about a specific KFC restaurant.

## Import
To use the `Kfc` card in your Boxel application, import it from the specified module:

```typescript
import { Kfc } from '../kfc';
```

## Usage as a Field
You can use the `Kfc` card as a field within a consuming card or field. For example:

```typescript
import { CardDef, containsMany } from '@cardstack/skills/Skill/boxel-development';

export class MyCard extends CardDef {
  @containsMany(Kfc)
  kfcLocations: Kfc[];
}
```

In this example, the `MyCard` card definition contains a `kfcLocations` field that can hold one or more `Kfc` cards.

## Template Usage
To display the `Kfc` card within a template, you can use the following syntax:

```handlebars
<@fields.kfcLocations as |kfc|>
  <Kfc @model={{kfc}} />
</@fields.kfcLocations>
```

This will render the `Kfc` card for each item in the `kfcLocations` array.
